title: KYC Continuous Monitoring
app: KYC vendor API logs, case management
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype="kyc:screening"
| where match(alert_type,"(sanctions|pep|adverse)") AND disposition="open"
| stats latest(_time) as last_alert, values(match_score) as scores by customer_id, case_id
| where last_alert < relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| sort last_alert
description: Detects adverse media, sanctions hits, or identity churn post-onboarding.
value: Detects adverse media, sanctions hits, or identity churn post-onboarding.
implementation: Refresh high-risk customers on schedule; integrate watchlist updates; escalate unresolved alerts > SLA.
visualization: Table (aging KYC alerts), Line chart (screening volume), Single value (open PEP cases).
knownFalsePositives: KYC screening hits on common names, data vendor delays, or bulk refreshes; validate against case management state before auto-escalation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: KYC vendor API logs, case management.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="kyc:screening"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Refresh high-risk customers on schedule; integrate watchlist updates; escalate unresolved alerts > SLA.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="kyc:screening"
| where match(alert_type,"(sanctions|pep|adverse)") AND disposition="open"
| stats latest(_time) as last_alert, values(match_score) as scores by customer_id, case_id
| where last_alert < relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| sort last_alert
```

Understanding this SPL

**KYC Continuous Monitoring** — Detects adverse media, sanctions hits, or identity churn post-onboarding.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="kyc:screening"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): KYC vendor API logs, case management. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: kyc:screening. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="kyc:screening". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(alert_type,"(sanctions|pep|adverse)") AND disposition="open"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by customer_id, case_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where last_alert < relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (aging KYC alerts), Line chart (screening volume), Single value (open PEP cases).
grandmaExplanation: In Financial Services, we pay close attention to "KYC Continuous Monitoring" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
